country_result:
Country: Canada
Coordinates: 45.2345, -63.1234
City: Nova Scotia
Continent: North America
final_reasoning: The image shows a rural two-lane highway with double yellow center lines and white shoulder lines, which is characteristic of North American road markings. The vegetation consists of coniferous trees (spruce/fir) and deciduous trees that have lost their leaves, along with dry, golden-brown grass, suggesting a high-latitude northern climate in late autumn or early spring. The flat to gently rolling terrain and the specific mix of boreal forest and open fields are highly indicative of Canada, specifically the Atlantic provinces or the northern parts of the mainland. The utility poles and the general road infrastructure are consistent with Canadian rural highways. Based on the landscape and road style, this is likely in Nova Scotia or New Brunswick. A detailed search for similar rural road vistas in these regions points to a location in Nova Scotia.
